page: Amazon Web Services m6id.12xlarge is a general-purpose server powered by an Intel Xeon 8375C processor with x86_64 architecture. It features 48 vCPUs and 24 physical cores running at 3.5 GHz, supported by 192.0 GB of DDR4 memory at 3 GHz. The instance includes 2850 GB of local NVMe SSD storage and a baseline network bandwidth of 19 Gbps, running on the Nitro hypervisor.

Benchmark results indicate strong multi-core and specialized processing capabilities. The server achieved a strong Geekbench multi-core score of 15999.0 and a strong Passmark CPU score of 48532.6, alongside strong performance in image processing and CPU encryption. Static web serving throughput is also strong at 41.18 GB/sec. However, while small-model LLM inference is strong, performance drops to average for medium models and becomes weak for large models, showing a clear tradeoff for heavy AI workloads without dedicated GPU acceleration.

The inclusion of 2850 GB of local NVMe SSD storage provides high resource density, making it a qualitatively cost-effective option for workloads requiring high-speed local scratch space without the added cost of external block storage. This hardware profile is well-suited for database operations, static web serving, software compilation, and general-purpose enterprise applications that benefit from balanced compute, memory, and fast local storage.
description: Amazon Web Services m6id.12xlarge is an x86_64 general-purpose server featuring 48 vCPUs and 24 physical cores powered by an Intel Xeon 8375C processor running at 3.5 GHz. It is equipped with 192.0 GB of DDR4 memory, 2850 GB of local NVMe SSD storage, and 19 Gbps baseline network bandwidth. Operating on the Nitro hypervisor, this instance delivers strong multi-core performance, as evidenced by a Geekbench multi-core score of 15999.0 and a Passmark CPU score of 48532.6. It also shows strong capabilities in static web serving and small-model LLM inference, though performance degrades to weak for large-model inference due to the lack of GPU acceleration. The bundled high-capacity local NVMe storage offers excellent qualitative cost efficiency for data-intensive tasks. This server is optimized for databases, software compilation, static web serving, and general-purpose enterprise workloads.
og_description: Amazon Web Services m6id.12xlarge is a general-purpose server with 48 Intel Xeon vCPUs, 192 GB DDR4 RAM, and 2850 GB local NVMe SSD storage, delivering strong multi-core and web serving performance.
meta_description: Amazon Web Services m6id.12xlarge features 48 Intel vCPUs, 192 GB RAM, and 2850 GB NVMe SSD storage for balanced general-purpose workloads.
tagline: A balanced general-purpose server featuring dedicated Intel Xeon processors, high-capacity local NVMe SSD storage, and strong multi-core performance.
bullet_points: Intel Xeon 8375C processor with 48 vCPUs and 192 GB DDR4 RAM
Bundled 2850 GB of high-speed local NVMe SSD storage
Strong multi-core performance in Geekbench and Passmark benchmarks
19 Gbps baseline network bandwidth on the Nitro hypervisor
Suited for databases, web serving, and software compilation
